segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: By mid-morning the ironwoods began to thin. The air lost its tang of cold iron and sap; warm earth came up instead -- mud, wet moss, the faint yeasty breath of leaf-rot.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin walked center, the witch-knot ribbon Nyxara had tied at his wrist dull as old wine in the daylight. Every so often it ticked with the gentlest tug, as if a spider had plucked a single strand of web and let it sing.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not a direction -- just a proof of distance, of attention. Someone, somewhere, still watching.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex ranged left, favoring her wrapped ribs but moving like a rumor. Durgan took the right, ice-blue eyes on the tree line, shadow obedient as a good dog at noon.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Aldric walked rear -- medical bag across one shoulder, eyes moving between the road ahead and the tree line Durgan was watching, doing his own quiet accounting of threats.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The world smelled almost domestic for the first time since the mountain -- woodsmoke drift from somewhere far downslope; a farm's latrine, honest and rank. Civilization as scent.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They hit the ruts of the old Merchant Road by noon -- two pale crescents pressed into clay, puddles winking with sky. Ahead, a broken cart tilted against a ditch. A kettle still hung from its hook, long gone cold. Chicken feathers lay stuck to sap on a fallen branch. Someone had left in a hurry.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Tracks,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said, crouching.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Two families. One cartwheel cracked, they ditched it. Southbound -- same as us."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She sniffed the air, a feral, automatic habit.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Yesterday, maybe sunrise."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The ribbon on Caelin's wrist gave that whisper-tug again. He didn't look at it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He touched the bronzed ward-disc Serana had pressed into his palm when she left -- the surface etched with a line of secular intention, protective not because a god blessed it, but because a stubborn woman had. The metal held a little warmth.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It steadied him more than he liked admitting.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Company,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan murmured.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They saw the woman first -- a farmer, hair gone wild, dress torn at the hem, one arm around a boy not more than seven and the other around a bundle that might have been bread or a baby.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She stood in the road's center, frozen like a doe, staring at the black smear moving along the ditch shadow -- a scout's shade, thin as spilled ink.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The thing reached for the boy's feet.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan moved.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He didn't think. He went.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: One clean sprint that ate the distance, one boot heel that slammed the shadow flat against bright clay, and then the soldier's rhythm took over -- draw, slash, step; blade in a short arc that cut where light and ground met, the place shadows anchor.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The shade recoiled, hissing without a mouth, and shivered itself thin to slither away.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan planted a second cut that pinned it like a nail through silk.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Run,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he told the woman without looking back.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Don't stop until you see a palisade."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She didn't argue. The boy stared at Durgan's face like he'd never seen a man before; then he was swept down the road by his mother's panic.

segments.speaker: aldric
segments.text: "Check the boy for shock when you stop!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Aldric called after them.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "He'll be quiet -- that's the wrong kind of quiet!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He watched them go, then looked at the smear on the road where the shade had been.

segments.speaker: aldric
segments.text: "That's different from what came through the vault."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He said it without emphasis, matter-of-fact.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "The Veln's Post thing was bigger. Older. This is a forward scout."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex's eyes flicked to Durgan when it was done. Approval was not her currency, but it showed anyway, quick as light on a blade.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin exhaled a breath he hadn't known he held. The scale in his forearm said nothing -- no sting, no warning. The world smelled of crushed grass and the metallic, coin-thin scent the shade had left behind.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan's nose was dry when he wiped it with the back of his hand. He checked anyway. Habit. Waiting.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Thanks,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan sheathed his blade.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Keep moving."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They did.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They smelled tar before they saw the barricade.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The road bent between two low knuckles of granite where ironwoods had failed to root; smoke lay in the turn like old breath. Four horses stood side by side, heads down, lather drying white on their necks.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A rope-drag of dead brush spanned the road, glistening with black pitch. Six men in leather and scale waited behind it, crossbows already braced on the rocks.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: One wore a red kerchief at his throat; one had the lazy stance of someone who'd killed often enough to be bored by it.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Afternoon,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: the bored one said.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "You match a description."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex's mouth twitched.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Let me guess. 'Tall, cursed, and bad at avoiding trouble.'"

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Something like that."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The bored man's gaze never left Caelin's arm.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Bounty says alive. Your friends may sulk off or not. Choice is a luxury but I'm magnanimous."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Aldric, standing at the back of the group, set his medical bag down on the road with the quiet deliberateness of a man who has been in enough of these situations to know that the bag stays where it can be reached quickly. He folded his hands and said nothing. He was not going to sulk off.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin's scale warmed. Not a flare; a gathering. He counted breaths, counted costs. The pitch-brush smelled like shipyards and siege ladders. Tar's flash point lived in his hands if he asked for it.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Crossbow pivots,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex murmured from the corner of her mouth.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Two right, one left, leader center. Drop center, I delete right. You still standing?"

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Standing,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said. He flexed his hand. The lattice tugged back, a warning twine, but his body had learned new shapes for pain.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan's voice stayed flat.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Last chance. Walk away."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The red-kerchief man smiled with his eyes.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "No."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Time folded.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex moved first. A silver line crossed sunlight -- her throwing knife -- and the central bowman jerked as if a wasp stung his eye, then folded to the dust.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin breathed in. Heat gathered behind his ribs the way a forge gathers rightness; he pictured not fire but temperature, a widow's kiss that rushed through air without flame. His palm lifted.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The pitch line sighed, then whoomph -- tar flared in a low, fast curtain. Black smoke stamped the sky. The barricade twisted and fell.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Bolts sang toward them anyway -- two, three. One hissed past Caelin's ear. One struck stone and cracked like a promise. One took Durgan's sleeve and left a slice of red without slowing him.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He was already in their faces, blades walking the short economy of a soldier trained for close work. Two men went down with groans that would fade or not; he left them breathing on purpose.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Pain pealed through Caelin's arm -- more bell than blade. He took it. Let it ring his bones. He dropped to one knee and pulled air through his teeth, felt the lattice complain, then settle into the old, ugly baseline.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex hit the right-hand pair like consequence. First man's wrist -- snapped; second's kneecap -- cut; she drove her pommel into a temple and let him sleep out his poor choices.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The last bounty hunter -- the bored one -- dropped his crossbow, raised his hands, and smiled again with nothing to smile about.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Mercy?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan's knife was at his throat.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "You'll leave a message."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Ah. To whom?"

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Whoever pays you. Tell them you saw the fire and lived. Tell them next time you won't."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The man swallowed, his Adam's apple pushing the blade.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I can do polite."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Do it farther away."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan stepped back. The shadow under his boots stayed ordinary in the full hammer of noon.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They dragged the pitch brush off the road with their boots. The world was tar reek and burned horsehair and the sour tang of someone's fear-sweat.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Aldric went straight to Durgan.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Sleeve up."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "It's a scratch."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Sleeve up."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan pushed up the sleeve. The bolt had opened a four-inch line across the forearm -- clean, not deep. Aldric cleaned it with something from a small flask that smelled medicinal and unpleasant, bound it with three quick turns of linen, and tied it off without ceremony.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Keep it dry. It won't."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He looked at Caelin on one knee.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "How's the arm?"

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "The same."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "That's not an answer."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "It's the answer I have."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Aldric looked at him for a moment with the expression of a man who'd heard that particular response in that particular tone before, from men who were about to find out it wasn't enough. He let it go. For now.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex wiped her knife on a patch of clean grass and cocked a brow at Caelin.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "How many of those,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, nodding at the blackened barricade,

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "before you fall down and I have to carry you?"

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I'm not sure,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin admitted.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Fewer than we'd like. More than yesterday."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Progress,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, and almost smiled.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan wiped his sleeve. No blood on his hand this time. He exhaled once, like a man setting a weight down where no one would notice.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They took the horses nobody wanted to keep and rode until the light went copper.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Evening found them on the lip of an old wound -- an earthquake's mouth split months ago or centuries, who could say. A basalt bridge arched the gap in a single impossible span, black as a raven's back, edges beveled to a craftsman's vanity.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Wind lived down there. It smelled of cold stone and something old and salty, though the sea lay weeks away.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: At the near end of the bridge, someone had carved a sigil into a waist-high stone: a circle doubled back on itself, nine spokes, the tenth line severed. Thornik would have told them the lineage of the chisel marks.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: In his absence, Caelin only felt seen. The scale warmed, shyly. Vex touched the symbol with two fingers and let the gesture be a greeting.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Halfway across, the light changed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not dimmer -- stranger. The sun still bled out across the world, but the space over the span held a second dusk of its own, a hush laid over air. Caelin tasted metal on his tongue. The ribbon at his wrist gave a single bright pluck, like a harpstring in a room with no hands.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Words raised from the stone, not carved but standing -- letters the eye only loved if it looked from the side.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Speak true or speak not at all.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex stopped dead.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I hate bridges that talk."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "It's a ward,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said. He took out Serana's disc. The secular line etched into bronze caught the odd light and answered in a soft, stubborn glow. The sense of it wasn't holy, not anymore. It was made strong by work. By intention. I will keep what I can.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He cleared his throat.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "We are crossing to the plateau. We intend to harm no one who does not try to harm us. We seek a prison that isn't a prison."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He paused. Laughed once at himself.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "We're in over our heads."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The bridge listened. Said nothing. But the pressure eased enough to breathe.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Fine,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said, and stepped into the ward's hush. She could have lied to it with panache. She chose not to.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I am very tired,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she informed the air.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I am very hungry. I do not want to die before I stab whoever designed this road."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The hush let her pass.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan hesitated a fraction. His shadow bled one inch toward the parapet, stopped as if it had hit glass, and recoiled like a burned animal. The smell of singed wool flicked the air then died, leaving the clean ache of wind.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I will not hurt them,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said to no one and everyone.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Not while I am me."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The ward accepted that as truth.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Aldric stepped up to the ward's edge and stood there a moment with his hands at his sides. He didn't look like a man performing sincerity. He looked like a man who'd decided what was true and was simply reporting it.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I came to find out what killed thirty-seven men,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "And if there's anything left to do about it."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The hush considered this and let him through.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They stood at the bridge's crown and looked north. The world beyond rolled away in bruised gold: scrub, broken stone, low thorn.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: And there, like a nail driven into the horizon, lay a black plateau shouldering out of the world -- the Depthspire rise, not yet the thing itself, but the country of it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Above, a pale aurora wrote thin curtains across the sky where no such lights should run.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A square of folded paper fell out of Caelin's pack and landed against his boot.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex's hand hit a dagger before he'd bent to pick it up. Durgan's blades whispered half-free and went still when nothing moved to justify it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The note was ordinary to the point of insolence -- cheap stock, black ink, a smell of old lavender as if it had slept too long in an honest drawer.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin opened it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Your pace is sufficient. Keep the road's left edge when the basalt goes to clay; the right is mined by men who will not remember why they laid the charges. I will not wait long at the first watch-tower. -- L

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex made a face that belonged to someone who'd bitten a green apple.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Divination and manners,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Pick one."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin folded the note, slid it away.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "He's ahead of us."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Or behind and being clever about it."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex shaded her eyes against a wind that didn't need shading.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Either way, I vote we don't sleep on the bridge."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Aldric looked at the note Caelin had pocketed, then at the plateau on the horizon, then said nothing. He'd stopped asking who L was. The answer was somewhere north of them and he'd find out when they got there.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They finished the span, and the hush let them go. On the far side, someone had piled three stones into a cairn you'd miss if you blinked.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin set Serana's disc on the top for a breath, warmed it with the heat the scale made when it was patient, and took it back. The night smelled of sage crushed under boots and basalt cooling after day.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They made camp in the lee of a boulder where the wind could sing without tearing the blankets loose. The ribbon at Caelin's wrist lay quiet. The ward-disc held its stubborn warmth.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan set himself where he could see both the bridge and their fire; when his nose bled, he turned his head and let the dark drink it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Aldric sat with his back to the boulder and his bag between his feet and looked north, doing whatever arithmetic he'd been doing since Veln's Post and not sharing the result.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Sleep came as a courtesy, not a gift.
